description: Public-domain OSHA workplace-safety publications that make up the OSHA corpus for fedbench. All documents are works of the US federal government and not subject to copyright (17 USC §105). Each entry pins a URL and a SHA-256 checksum so a fork can verify it received exactly the same bytes the eval was run against. Chosen to give a retrieval profile distinct from the Medicare corpus — procedural and list-heavy rather than prose-heavy, with concrete equipment, planning, and screening requirements rather than benefits policy.

documents.title: Personal Protective Equipment
documents.scope: OSHA 3151-02R (2023). Employer responsibilities for hazard assessment and PPE selection — eye, face, head, foot, hand, hearing, respiratory protection. Reference for the equipment-and-rule shape of OSHA standards. Concrete, list-heavy retrieval target.

documents.title: How to Plan for Workplace Emergencies and Evacuations
documents.scope: OSHA 3088 (2001, revised). Emergency Action Plan (EAP) requirements, evacuation routes, alarm systems, employee training, and post-evacuation accounting. Heavy on planning checklists — strongest retrieval contrast with the Medicare corpus's prose-heavy benefits language.

documents.title: Medical Screening and Surveillance Requirements in OSHA Standards: A Guide
documents.scope: OSHA 3162-01R (2014). When OSHA standards require medical exams, surveillance schedules, exposure-trigger thresholds (e.g., asbestos, lead, benzene). Bridges occupational-safety procedural language to a healthcare-adjacent vocabulary, useful for testing whether retrieval generalizes across federal-policy domains.
